samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a).</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b).</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">16</parameter>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">16</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The module performs unsigned 16-bit multiplication using shift and accumulate operations. It utilizes a shift count register (i) to control the multiplication process and a completion flag (done_r) to indicate the end of the operation.
    On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated. If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the shift count register (i) is set to 0. If the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17, it increments by 1. If the start signal is inactive (!start), the shift count register (i) is reset to 0.
    On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated. If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is set to 0. If the shift count register (i) is equal to 16, it indicates the completion of the multiplication operation, and the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is set to 1. If the shift count register (i) is equal to 17, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is reset to 0.
    The shift and accumulate operation is performed on every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n). If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) are reset to 0. If the start signal is active (start), the module starts the multiplication operation. When the shift count register (i) is 0, the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) are stored in the respective registers (areg and breg). For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, the product register (yout_r) accumulates the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.
    </implementation>
</module>
